title: Health insurance plans
description: Practice your understanding of different health insurance plans

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people get health insurance through their job. This is called an employer-provided health plan.

** Who typically pays for the cost of an employer-provided health plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: both the employer and the employee

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people get health insurance through a government sponsored program such as Medicaid or Medicare.

**Who typically pays for the cost of a government sponsored health plan?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the individual receiving the health insurance

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people purchase health insurance on their own, rather than getting it through their employer or the government. 

**Who typically pays for the cost of an individual health plan?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the individual receiving the health insurance

assessment_items.question_content:
Individual health plans are insurance policies that people can buy for themselves and their families, instead of getting insurance through their job or the government.

**Which of the following is a potential benefit of an individual health plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: low premiums for people with pre-existing health issues

assessment_items.question_content:
Government-provided health plans are insurance policies funded by the government, such as Medicaid and the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

**Which of the following is a benefit of government-provided health plans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: available to everyone regardless of age

assessment_items.question_content:
An employer-provided health plan is a health insurance policy offered by a person's job to help cover medical expenses and keep their employees healthy.

**What is one benefit of an employer-provided health plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: your employer may pay for part or all of the cost.
you will automatically qualify for Medicaid
you will have no out-of-pocket costs
you can choose the plan that best meets your needs

assessment_items.question_content:
Health insurance is important to have, as it helps us pay for medical care. There are different types of health insurance, and each type of health insurance has its own costs and benefits.

**Which type of health insurance plan typically has the *highest* monthly premiums?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Health insurance is important to have, as it helps us pay for medical care. There are different types of health insurance, and each type of health insurance has its own costs and benefits.

**Which type of health insurance plan typically has the *lowest* monthly premiums?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Health insurance is important to have, as it helps us pay for medical care. There are different types of health insurance, and each type of health insurance has its own costs and benefits.

**How does an individual health plan compare to all others, when it comes to *cost*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: it is *less* expensive than government plans, but *more* expensive than employer plans

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are a $22$-year-old who just got a full-time job. Your employer offers a health insurance plan, but you are unsure if it is the best option for you. You are considering different types of health insurance plans and their costs. 

**Which of the following is the least expensive option for you in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Imagine you are a $25$-year-old recent college graduate who just started their first full-time job at a small company. The company does not offer health insurance benefits and you are no longer eligible for your parents' health insurance plan.

**Which of the following options will work for you in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Imagine you are a $24$-year-old who works part-time at a local grocery store. Health insurance is a benefit offered at your job, but only to full-time employees. You don't have any pre-existing health conditions, and your income is low. 

**Which of the following is the least expensive option in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
